sections.paragraph:
ἔγκαρπος, ον (ἐν + καρπός; Soph., Pla. et al.; PTebt 815, 6; 55 [III b.c.]; PFlor 369, 13; Jer 38:12) pert. to being conducive to a bountiful yield, fruitful, fig. (Ael. Aristid. 46 p. 404 D.: λόγος ἔ.; Maximus Tyr. 34, 4b βίος) ἀνάλυσις a fruitful departure 1 Cl 44:5; μνεία fruitful remembrance 56:1.—DELG s.v. καρπός.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 273
